name: Portabella Mushroom Risotto
recipeIngredient: Salt and black pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the mushrooms: Remove stems and set aside for broth. Clean mushroom caps with a damp cloth. Combine olive oil, shallot, balsamic vinegar, salt, and pepper. Toss mushroom caps to coat well.
2. Cook mushrooms: Grill or broil until tender (about 10 minutes). Cut into small chunks and set aside.
3. Prepare mushroom broth: Mince reserved stems. Sauté garlic and shallot in olive oil until translucent. Add stems and sauté a few more minutes. Pour in red wine, evaporate most of it, then add stock. Simmer uncovered for 25-30 minutes or until liquid is reduced by one-fourth.
4. Prepare risotto: Cook onion in oil until soft but not browned. Add rice, stirring until coated. Cook until toasted aroma develops. Stir in salt and pepper. Gradually add reserved mushroom stock (and additional stock if needed), stirring frequently and allowing most of the stock to be absorbed before adding more. Finish cooking when rice is nearly tender and liquid is absorbed.
5. Stir in remaining olive oil and reserved mushrooms before serving.
